surface.name: Astro CLI local Airflow quickstart
surface.selection_basis: Under the workshop policy, the official local Astro CLI route takes priority over cloud onboarding: it requires no Astro account, initializes an example DAG, and can run Airflow locally in standalone mode without Docker or Podman.

alternatives_considered.name: Run your first DAG with the Astro CLI
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This is an alternative for developers who skip the onboarding template or GitHub connection and adds CLI installation, browser login, and local project commands.

alternatives_considered.name: Run your first DAG with GitHub Actions
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The onboarding guide presents GitHub Actions as an alternative after the developer skips its integrated GitHub route.

alternatives_considered.name: Astro local development
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Local development is a later development workflow and does not establish first success on the managed Astro platform.

entry_point.developer_intent: Install the Astro CLI, initialize an Astro project, and start its example DAG in a local Airflow environment.
assumed_prior_state: No Astro user account, Organization, Workspace, Deployment, project repository, DAG deployment, Airflow run, billing method, or trial state is assumed.
A valid email address, browser, and network connection are available; a GitHub account is recommended but not assumed.
No account details were submitted, email or external identity was authorized, cloud resources were created, repository was connected, code was deployed, or DAG was run during research.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: Start an Astro project in a local Airflow environment.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: The generated example DAG is available in a locally running Airflow UI.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The `astro dev start --standalone` command completes and the Airflow UI is accessible at `https://localhost:8080/`.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The local Airflow UI is available at `https://localhost:8080/` after startup completes.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: This is the earliest functioning no-account Astro environment; deployment to managed Astro is a later cloud route.

prerequisites.requirement: Provide a valid email address and create a password to create an Astro user account.
Create an Astro Organization and first Workspace after the user account is created.
Have a GitHub account for the recommended integrated repository route; otherwise use the documented CLI or GitHub Actions continuation.
Allow access to the Astro, API, image, authentication, update, install, Datakin, and organization runtime domains listed by the guide if the organization network restricts them.

primary_path.action: Open the official Astro CLI installation guide.
primary_path.success_signal: The Homebrew installation route is documented.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Use the documented Windows or Linux route on another OS.

details: This installs the Astro CLI and its default local container runtime on macOS.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The guide documents tap and update recovery for Homebrew failures.

primary_path.success_signal: A valid Astro CLI version appears.

primary_path.action: Create an empty directory, enter it, and run `astro dev init`.
primary_path.output: An Astro project with `dags/exampledag.py` is generated.
primary_path.success_signal: The standard project files and example DAG exist.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Run the command in an empty folder.

primary_path.action: Run `astro dev start --standalone` from the project directory.
details: Standalone mode runs Airflow directly in a virtual environment without Docker or Podman.
primary_path.output: The local Airflow webserver and scheduler start.
primary_path.success_signal: Startup completes and prints local access information.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Wait for environment setup; use documented troubleshooting if startup fails.

primary_path.action: Open `https://localhost:8080/` and inspect the generated example DAG.
details: Local credentials are `admin` and `admin` by default.
primary_path.output: The local Airflow UI and example DAG are visible.
primary_path.success_signal: The example DAG appears in the DAG list.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Check `astro dev logs` if the UI is unavailable.

branches.condition: The developer chooses a sample project
branches.path: Select Generative AI, ETL, Learning Airflow, or dbt on Astro, then confirm or change it during Deployment review.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes the example DAG and may add template-specific run parameters, while retaining the hosted DAG-run terminal.

branches.condition: The developer selects None or skips GitHub
branches.path: Create the Deployment and follow the Astro CLI or GitHub Actions first-DAG guide to deploy an example DAG.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Replaces integrated repository creation and deployment with a separate documented toolchain.

branches.path: Provide the GitHub owner and repository information so Astro can create a private repository from the selected template and deploy it.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds an external GitHub identity and repository authorization boundary.

branches.path: Keep the defaults or edit the parameters, then select Trigger.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds an optional configuration choice immediately before execution.

branches.path: Subscribe to the Astro pay-as-you-go Developer plan through AWS Marketplace instead of relying only on the direct trial path.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Introduces an external marketplace and existing AWS billing configuration.

friction_gates.description: An Astro user account is required before the first-DAG onboarding guide.
The live account application is client-rendered, so its exact fields and state transitions are not publicly inspectable.
Onboarding asks for use context, a sample template, Git connection choice, Deployment name, provider, and region.
The recommended repository path depends on a GitHub account and repository connection; skipping it changes the deployment route.
The developer must wait for the template deployment to succeed and for the triggered DAG to finish before viewing result logs.
The trial provides 14 days and $20 in credits; a payment method is required for additional functionality or continued access after the trial.

sources.title: Run your first dag on Astro | Astronomer Documentation
sections_used: Step 2: Create your Organization and Workspace
Step 4: Set up your Astro Deployment
Step 5: Run your first dag on Astro

sources.title: Start your Astro trial | Astronomer Documentation
sections_used: Subscribe through a cloud provider marketplace

sources.title: Astro
evidence_supported: Official live account and cloud application endpoint
No publicly readable server-rendered account form or continuation states

sources.title: Run your Astro project in a local Airflow environment with the CLI

uncertainties.question: What exact signup fields, identity alternatives, validation messages, verification steps, and post-submit state are currently shown by the Astro application?
uncertainties.impact: The account segment cannot be represented atomically from public evidence.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The written trial guide names email and password, but the linked application is client-rendered and does not expose the full live sequence publicly.

uncertainties.question: Which onboarding template, provider, and region are preselected for a new trial account?
uncertainties.impact: The exact click path and resulting example DAG can vary.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The guide lists the available choices but does not identify universal defaults.

excluded_after_success: Developing a custom Astro project, writing another DAG, installing the Astro CLI for local testing, CI/CD workflows, additional Deployments, production resource sizing, observability, team administration, and paid continuation occur after the first example DAG has finished and its logs are visible.
